violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned and sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was observed exceeding 200 parts per million (ppm) at the time of inspection. The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Chlorine sanitizer solution was fixed to 100 ppm at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

1 door glass reach in was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods in unit were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or lower. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Salmon were observed thawing in the 1 door glass reach in within it's original reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood within ROP packaging must be completely removed from packaging upon thawing. Salmon was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection, and thawing practices reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
